inputs.symptom: a thermal spike in the core

inputs.technician_message: module-2: oil weeping at a joint
module-2: current ripple on the output
module-1: an audible whine under load

inputs.procedure: Replace module-1's front_bearing. Drain the cavity and let it settle for 10s. Press the old part out with puller-A and press the replacement in. Re-torque the retainer to 14 Nm in 3 passes. Bleed the line and run the bleed-then-seat routine.

Replace module-1's rotor. Lock the shaft and de-energize, holding 8s. Release the drive and draw the old part out with hex-6. Seat the replacement and torque the mount to 18 Nm in 4 passes. Spin-test, then run the phase-align-B routine.

Replace module-2's capacitor_bank. De-energize and discharge the bus, holding 5s. Disconnect the 6 leads and lift out the old part with driver-3. Fit the replacement and torque the terminals to 21 Nm in 2 passes. Run the torque-stage-3 routine and verify.

Replace module-2's encoder. Power down and unmount the old part with hex-2. Fit the replacement and snug the 8 screws in a clockwise sequence. Torque to 16 Nm in 2 passes. Align with the null-balance routine and confirm the reading.

Replace module-3's housing_cover. Vent the bay and back off the 4 retaining bolts in a star sequence. Lift out the old part and seat the replacement using driver-1. Torque the bolts to 12 Nm across 3 passes. Finish with the seat-and-lock routine.

Replace module-1's shaft_seal. Drain the cavity and let it settle for 15s. Press the old part out with clamp-2 and press the replacement in. Re-torque the retainer to 9 Nm in 4 passes. Bleed the line and run the purge-cycle routine.
